title: Best Wine Bars in the LES
blurb: Natural, low-intervention, and the kind of places where the person behind the bar actually wants to talk.

places.name: Ten Bells
places.desc: A tiny cave of a wine bar with an ever-changing natural list. No cocktails, no beer. Bartenders know the producers by name. Arrive before 8pm.

places.name: Wildair
places.tag: Natural wine, small plates, loud room
places.desc: The louder, more casual sibling of Contra. The wine list is serious, the food is unfussy, the room gets packed on weekends. Snag a seat at the bar if you're solo.

places.name: Attaboy
places.tag: No menu — just tell them your vibe
places.desc: Describe what you feel like drinking and they'll make something you didn't know you wanted. No menu, no attitude. The door is easy to miss.

places.name: Parcelle Wine Bar
places.desc: Half wine shop, half bar. Buy a bottle retail and drink it there with a small corkage, or order from the list. The selection rewards curiosity. Small bites pair well.

places.name: June Wine Bar
places.desc: A low-key Cobble Hill wine bar that punches well above its neighborhood-spot status. The list changes constantly and the staff actually drinks this stuff.
